metadata.description: Stomatopod crustaceans are important predators in tropical marine environments. Though the adults are benthic cavity-dwellers, their young are planktonic as larvae. A series of light trap transects at Cook‚Äö√Ñ√¥s Bay in Moorea, French Polynesia taken over the course of one lunar cycle evaluated the spatial patterns of abundance and diversity of stomatopod larvae. A total of 813 stomatopod larvae were collected and identified with Pullosquilla sp. larvae being the most abundant taxa, composing over 70% of the total catch. In the back reef habitat, pullosquillids and diversity were highest to either side of the lagoonal channel. In the bay habitat, Pullosquillids and diversity were highest at the mouth of the bay, and lowest at the headwaters. No consistent vertical stratification was observed in the bay.
metadata.title: A light trap survey of stomatopod larvae at Cook's Bay, Moorea
